### Post by jollemeyer on 2007-05-02
I have Kubuntu Feisty installed.

My graphics card is as follows:
nVidia Corporation NV18 [GeForce4 MX 440SE AGP 8x], with proprietary driver installed
3D acceleration is enabled an working fine

glxinfo | grep -i direct confirms that Direct Rendering is enabled.

My xorg.conf is as follows:
```

joerg@Phoenix:~$ cat /etc/X11/xorg.conf
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
```

I installed beryl and beryl-manager as defined in this thread directly from the beryl-project [http://wiki.beryl-project.org/wiki/Install_Beryl_on_Ubuntu_Feisty_with_nVidia#Kubuntu]("http://wiki.beryl-project.org/wiki/Install_Beryl_on_Ubuntu_Feisty_with_nVidia#Kubuntu") wiki:
```
 Kubuntu

1. Open a terminal. Execute:

sudo echo "Beryl & nVidia installation script for Kubuntu"

(this one line requires your password so that, the next text paste is uninterrupted.)

2. Copy and paste all the text into the Terminal in one action. [Select all the text. Then middle button click in terminal]

sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.backup.beryl-script
sudo cp /etc/apt/sources.list /etc/apt/sources.list.backup.beryl-script
echo "deb http://ubuntu.beryl-project.org feisty main
deb-src http://ubuntu.beryl-project.org feisty main" | sudo tee -a /etc/apt/sources.list 
wget http://ubuntu.beryl-project.org/root@lupine.me.uk.gpg -O- | sudo apt-key add -
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get -y install beryl beryl-manager emerald-themes nvidia-glx nvidia-kernel-common aquamarine
sudo nvidia-xconfig --add-argb-glx-visuals
# Desktop icon
cp /usr/share/applications/beryl-manager.desktop ~/Desktop/beryl-manager.desktop 
# All Users
sudo cp /usr/share/applications/beryl-manager.desktop /usr/share/autostart/beryl-manager.desktop
# Single User
# cp /usr/share/applications/beryl-manager.desktop ~/.kde/Autostart/beryl-manager.desktop
echo -e "Logout now and then press \e[0;31mCTRL+ALT+BACKSPACE\e[0m to restart xorg"
echo "Done"


3. Now Logout and then press [CTRL+ALT+BACKSPACE] to restart xorg

```

I do get the following error message when starting beryl:

```
joerg@Phoenix:~$ beryl
**************************************************************

beryl: No manageable screens found on display :0
```


I already prepared a small script in my .kde/Autostart folder to autostart emerald as was stated in another thread:
```
#!/bin/sh
sleep 10
emerald &

``` 

But that didn't make any difference.

Being a noob, I have no idea if I use XGL or AIGLX.

Any help is appreciated.

### Post by lenooh on 2007-05-05
i installed ubuntu and then beryl. it worked without problems. then i installed kubuntu-desktop and booted into kde, ran beryl and got that error.

now it doesn't work in gnome eather, so it looks like kde messes something up.

take a look at this thread, the guy there says he fixed it:
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=2160211]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=2160211")

---

### Post by lenooh on 2007-05-05
ok, i found out why it was not working.

i don't know if it will work you too- i had to enable icons on the desktop (i always turn that off in kde, because i don't put things on my desktop)

so i enabled icons on the desktop, logged out, logged back in, did a
```
killall beryl-manager
```
than ran
```
beryl --replace
```
and it works :-)

good luck

---

### Post by rollsrice on 2007-05-06
An update:

Yesterday I was trying to delete different configurations files in my .kde folder, hoping to fix this thing, and then I just gave up and deleted the whole ~/.kde folder (I backed up my amarok collection though). And now? Beryl runs beautifully. Maybe it was because of the icons being disabled on the desktop - even I use this setting - but now after deleting the .kde folder, I again disabled the icons, and I've had no problems since then.

---

### Post by rollsrice on 2007-05-06
Looks like I spoke too soon . (Oops.) The earlier problem returned after I restarted my computer. 

It looks like disabling the desktop icons was creating the problem after all.

However, I think I found a fix for this maddening situation. Instead of starting beryl after logging on, I created a separate session that would start beryl automatically, as described here:

[http://www.biodesign.com.ar/blog/?p=23](http://www.biodesign.com.ar/blog/?p=23)

The only thing I changed was in the start-beryl.sh file, instead of 

> export KDEWM="/usr/bin/beryl-manager"

I put:

> export KDEWM="/usr/bin/beryl"

This way, I found, beryl started without any problems, even with the desktop icons disabled. Hope this is of use to someone.

---

### Post by Kratos on 2007-05-06
> **lenooh said:**
> ok, i found out why it was not working.

i don't know if it will work you too- i had to enable icons on the desktop (i always turn that off in kde, because i don't put things on my desktop)

so i enabled icons on the desktop, logged out, logged back in, did a
```
killall beryl-manager
```
than ran
```
beryl --replace
```
and it works :-)

good luck


Thank you! That was exactly what I needed! :)

If you absolutely hate having icons on the desktop, you could possibly change the permissions.
```

chmod 555 ~/Desktop
```

---

### Post by jollemeyer on 2007-05-07
rollsrice, lenooh, Kratos and all the others - thank you for your help.

Once you pointed into the correct direction, I identified that the behavior has already been reported as a  [bug]("http://bugs.beryl-project.org/ticket/101") to the beryl-project:

However, they claim it is a bug in KDE:

```
01/26/07 15:11:01 changed by QuinnStorm  ¶

    * status changed from reopened to closed.
    * resolution set to invalid.
    * component changed from beryl-core to not-our-bug.

This is a bug in kdesktop, it is selecting events on the root window that only a window manager (per ICCCM) is allowed to. This causes beryl to fail to start, failing to grab the events on the root window. The fix for now is to not hide icons, and this should be reported to kdesktop as a bug.
03/07/07 23:15:59 changed by djib ¶

I opened a bug report on bugs.kde.org : http://bugs.kde.org/show_bug.cgi?id=142666

Please vote for it. Thank you.

```

Anyway, the work around with enabling the desktop symbols works fine for me.
I will close this thread.

---
